basics.name: Sichi Yan
basics.summary: Master's student at Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU). Research focus: robot locomotion and morphology–control co-design. Seeking RA positions and PhD programs in robotics.

work.summary: Developed an end-to-end ROS2-based inference pipeline integrating a LoRA-adapted vision–language model with RGB-D sensing for near–real-time robotic scene understanding. Public materials omit customer-specific models, versions, and datasets.
highlights: Adapted a general-purpose VLM with LoRA on an in-house dataset for domain-specific classification in laboratory automation (task labels and taxonomy omitted publicly).
Built and deployed a ROS2 service node on Linux for VLM inference from camera streams; exact OS/ROS distro and GPU stack omitted on the public site.
Designed the full data collection, annotation, model adaptation, and deployment workflow at a high level.

publications.name: Human2Nav: Learning Crowd Navigation from Human Videos across Robots via Feasibility-Guided Flow Matching
publications.publisher: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA 2026)
publications.summary: A data-efficient framework learning robot crowd navigation policies directly from passive human videos via BEV representation and conditional flow matching. Test-time feasibility-guided inference adapts trajectories to heterogeneous robot kinematics without retraining. Deployed on differential-drive and quadrupedal robots across four crowd scenarios.

publications.name: CALA: Co-design with Accelerated Learning for Terrain-Specific Adaptive Locomotion
publications.summary: Submitted. A hierarchical bi-level co-design framework for terrain-specific adaptive locomotion.

portfolio.name: ROS2 VLM Inference
portfolio.description: End-to-end pipeline for robotic scene understanding using a LoRA-adapted vision–language model in a ROS2 service node with RGB-D sensing, for laboratory automation. Customer-specific models, versions, and task labels are omitted in public copy.
